title: SOAP Header Block
description: Schema describing an individual SOAP header block. Header blocks are discrete units of information within the SOAP header, each targeted at a specific SOAP node and optionally requiring processing by that node.

name.description: The local name of the header block element.

namespace.description: The XML namespace URI qualifying the header block element.

role.description: A URI identifying the SOAP node role that should process this header block. Standard roles include next, none, and ultimateReceiver.

mustUnderstand.description: Indicates whether the targeted SOAP node must process this header block. If true and the node cannot process it, a fault must be generated.

relay.description: Indicates whether this header block must be relayed to the next SOAP node if it is not processed. Only applicable to SOAP 1.2.

value.description: The content of the header block, which may be a string, object, or other structured data.
